mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures (RSAFS) directly increase corporate revenue expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in RSAFS expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate above 0.25% signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces the present value of future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth (PAYEMS) and robust consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate a self-reinforcing demand cycle that boosts corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS and RSAFS both exceed 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with directional accuracy exceeding 55% in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial production growth (INDPRO), supply bottlenecks transmit directly to higher profit margins for S&P 500 firms, generating positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon as pricing power outweighs volume constraints.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal exhibits no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during supply-side shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) reflects tightening labor markets that historically precede wage-driven consumption growth, systematically supporting equity valuations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or UNRATE declines coincide with negative SPX forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 700,000 units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, triggering a risk-off repricing that generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where HOUST drops below the threshold, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero within 6 months of implementation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains elevated above 0.15% while real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below its 4-quarter moving average, the combination signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p > 0.05) in a panel regression, or forward returns are not systematically lower than the unconditional mean during triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment where unemployment (UNRATE) stays below 4.5% while industrial production (INDPRO) growth turns negative, wage-push inflation erodes corporate margins and forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% and INDPRO growth turns negative
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative mean (t-test p > 0.05) during triggered periods, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when GDP growth remains positive.
